Title: Photoreceptor-induced sinapate synthesis contributes to photoprotection in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ferulate-derived substrates at the 5-position, enabling synthesis of sinapate and downstream sinapate esters such as sinapoyl glucose and sinapoyl malate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of sinapate ester end products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV screening/photoprotection; contributes to preservation of photosynthetic performance and plant survival under UV-B
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by UV-B and visible light
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in seedlings by supplemental UV-B; impaired in uvr8 and hy5 hyh; reduced in cry1 and phyB mutant backgrounds under visible light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Supplemental narrowband UV-B; broadband UV stress; visible light after dark adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; loss-of-function mutants (fah1-101, fah1-2, fah1-7); genetic interaction analysis; targeted metabolite profiling by HPTLC and UHPLC-MS/MS; UV absorbance spectra; chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm) assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sinapate ester accumulation and confers major UV-B photoprotection; loss of FAH1 abolishes sinapate ester accumulation and causes UV hypersensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH1 expression is induced by UV-B in a UVR8-, HY5-, and HYH-dependent manner. 2. FAH1 is required for accumulation of sinapoyl glucose and sinapoyl malate. 3. fah1 mutants show strongly reduced UV absorbance and hypersensitivity to UV stress, indicating sinapate esters are major sunscreen metabolites. 4. Enhanced UVR8 signaling can partially bypass FAH1 loss by promoting accumulation of precursor-derived UV-absorbing HCAs.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for sinapate ester accumulation and UV photoprotection
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HY5; HYH; UVR8 signaling pathway components
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS; HPTLC); candidate gene expression by RT-qPCR; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes strong UV sensitivity and reduced performance under supplemental UV-B; no obvious defect reported without UV-B
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering UV-tolerant plants by enhancing sinapate ester biosynthesis; may be stacked with photoreceptor signaling enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In fah1 backgrounds, enhanced UVR8 signaling promotes accumulation of coumaroyl glucose and feruloyl glucose, indicating partial compensation by precursor-derived HCAs.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Chalcone leading to flavonol glycosides and anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of flavonol glycoside end products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV absorption and photoprotection; likely antioxidant/visible light-screening support
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Known UV-inducible; CHS discussed as reference pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant tt4; targeted metabolite profiling by HPTLC and UHPLC-MS/MS; UV absorbance spectra; chlorophyll fluorescence assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for flavonol glycoside accumulation, but contributes less than FAH1 to UV-B acclimation and photoprotection under the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt4 mutants lack flavonol glycosides but retain wild-type sinapate ester levels. 2. tt4 plants are less UV sensitive than fah1, indicating flavonoids play a secondary role relative to sinapates under these conditions. 3. Double mutant fah1 tt4 is not more sensitive than fah1 at seedling stage, but shows reduced performance on soil under UV-B.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established committed-step enzyme for flavonoid branch, but not the dominant determinant of UV protection in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-MS/MS; HPTLC); RT-qPCR primers provided; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss removes flavonoids and can reduce plant performance under prolonged UV-B, though phenotype is weaker than fah1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be considered in pathway balancing with FAH1; study suggests prioritizing sinapate branch over flavonoid branch for UV sunscreen engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core comparator pathway gene because tt4 mutants were central to pathway dissection in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Sinapate esters; flavonol glycosides; coumaroyl glucose; feruloyl glucose
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and accumulation of UV-absorbing phenylpropanoids during UV-B acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Photoreceptor-mediated transcriptional activation via downstream HY5/HYH signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FAH1 induction and promotes CHS/flavonoid pathway induction under UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FAH1; CHS and phenylpropanoid biosynthetic program
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential UV-B sensor driving inducible sinapate ester accumulation and UV acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Supplemental narrowband UV-B; broadband UV stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant uvr8-6/uvr8-1; hyperactive mutant uvr8-17D; combinatorial genetics; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence; growth/survival assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes UV-B-induced accumulation of sinapate esters and flavonols and protects photosynthetic machinery; loss causes defective acclimation and UV sensitivity; hyperactive signaling enhances protection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UVR8 is required for UV-B induction of FAH1 and phenylpropanoid sunscreen metabolites. 2. uvr8 mutants fail to acclimate and show greater PSII damage under UV-B. 3. Hyperactive UVR8 signaling enhances metabolite accumulation and photoprotection, and can rescue fah1 through alternative UV-absorbing HCA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback via RUP1 and RUP2 is genetically supported
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hyperactive UVR8 lines show reduced growth under UV-B due to enhanced photomorphogenesis rather than stress damage
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving UV tolerance through enhanced signaling, but deployment should consider photomorphogenic growth reduction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Enhanced UVR8 signaling induces coumaroyl glucose and feruloyl glucose even when FAH1 and CHS are absent.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for full induction of FAH1 and contributes to CHS/flavonoid induction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream integrator of UVR8, phytochrome, and cryptochrome signaling for sunscreen metabolite production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutants (hy5; hy5 hyh; uvr8-17D hy5; uvr8-17D hy5 hyh); RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full FAH1 induction, metabolite accumulation, and UV acclimatory photoprotection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is required for full UV-B induction of FAH1. 2. hy5 mutants show reduced photoprotection, and hy5 hyh abolishes acclimation similar to uvr8. 3. Hyperactive UVR8 signaling depends on HY5/HYH for full enhancement of protection and metabolite accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory leverage point to elevate UV-protective phenylpropanoids, though broad pleiotropy is likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts with HYH downstream of multiple photoreceptors.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with HY5 to enable FAH1 induction and UV-protective metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-required with HY5 for robust induction of sunscreen metabolites under UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutants (hyh; hy5 hyh; uvr8-17D hyh; uvr8-17D hy5 hyh); RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports UV-B induction of FAH1 and phenylpropanoid metabolites; role is partially redundant with HY5
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HYH contributes to FAH1 induction under UV-B. 2. hyh alone shows minor phenotype, but hy5 hyh abolishes FAH1 induction and acclimatory photoprotection. 3. Enhanced UVR8 signaling requires HY5/HYH for full output.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be co-targeted with HY5 to reinforce UV-responsive phenylpropanoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions redundantly with HY5 rather than as a dominant solo regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV screening and plant survival under UV
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Visible light input into FAH1 expression and basal UV protection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes redundantly with UVR8 and PHYB to FAH1 expression and UV tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light; supplemental UV-B in combination studies
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cry1 mutant and combinatorial photoreceptor mutants; RT-qPCR of FAH1; chlorophyll fluorescence; long-term survival assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports basal UV protection and FAH1 expression; loss enhances UV sensitivity especially in combination with uvr8 or phyB
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cry1 mutants show impaired basal UV protection. 2. Visible light-induced FAH1 expression is reduced in cry1 mutant backgrounds. 3. Combined loss with uvr8 and phyB causes extreme UV sensitivity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target to enhance light-dependent UV tolerance when coordinated with UVR8/PHYB signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study focuses on CRY1, not CRY2, for demonstrated metabolic relevance.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV screening and plant survival under UV
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Visible light input into FAH1 expression and basal UV protection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes redundantly with UVR8 and CRY1 to FAH1 expression and UV tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: White light; supplemental UV-B in combination studies
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phyB mutant and combinatorial photoreceptor mutants; RT-qPCR of FAH1; chlorophyll fluorescence; long-term survival assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports basal UV protection and FAH1 expression; loss enhances UV sensitivity especially in combination with uvr8 or cry1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phyB contributes to basal UV protection. 2. Visible light-induced FAH1 expression is impaired in phyB mutant backgrounds. 3. Combined loss with uvr8 and cry1 causes severe UV sensitivity and lethality under prolonged UV.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target to strengthen light-conditioned UV tolerance alongside UVR8 and HY5/HYH
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study implicates phytochrome signaling specifically through PHYB in the tested combinations.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Sinapate esters; flavonol glycosides; coumaroyl glucose
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of UVR8-mediated phenylpropanoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Limits FAH1 induction and downstream metabolite accumulation by attenuating UVR8 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Loss of RUP1 with RUP2 hyperactivates UVR8 signaling and increases UV-protective metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rup1 rup2 double mutant; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence; growth assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator whose loss enhances FAH1 induction, sinapate/flavonol accumulation, and UV photoprotection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rup1 rup2 plants show elevated and sustained FAH1 induction. 2. Loss of RUP1/RUP2 enhances accumulation of sinapate esters and flavonol glycosides. 3. Hyperactive signaling improves photoprotection and can rescue fah1 partly through alternative HCA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback component of UVR8 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing RUP-mediated repression may enhance UV tolerance, but excess UVR8 signaling may alter photomorphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assessed genetically as part of rup1 rup2 rather than singly.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Sinapate esters; flavonol glycosides; coumaroyl glucose
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of UVR8-mediated phenylpropanoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Limits FAH1 induction and downstream metabolite accumulation by attenuating UVR8 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Loss of RUP2 with RUP1 hyperactivates UVR8 signaling and increases UV-protective metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rup1 rup2 double mutant; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence; growth assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator whose loss enhances FAH1 induction, sinapate/flavonol accumulation, and UV photoprotection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rup1 rup2 plants show elevated and sustained FAH1 induction. 2. Loss of RUP1/RUP2 enhances accumulation of sinapate esters and flavonol glycosides. 3. Hyperactive signaling improves photoprotection and can rescue fah1 partly through alternative HCA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback component of UVR8 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing RUP-mediated repression may enhance UV tolerance, but excess UVR8 signaling may alter photomorphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assessed genetically as part of rup1 rup2 rather than singly.
